claims.claim: `dynamic_semantic_stale_ms_for` should avoid a cliff across the recovery handover between fill 0.25 and 0.40, including the 0.35 release region.
claims.rationale: Existing tests `semantic_stale_recovery_handover_is_monotonic_and_micro_stutter_bounded` and `semantic_stale_release_035_to_045_fades_without_step_loss` verify monotonic bounded handover without changing live cadence.

claims.claim: `dynamic_surge_target_weight` should use smoothstep interpolation so fill 0.70 remains at the base surge target and fill 0.80 reaches the high-fill target.
claims.rationale: Existing tests `surge_target_weight_softens_when_fill_is_high` and `surge_target_weight_uses_soft_knee_across_taper` verify the endpoint behavior and soft-knee interpolation.

claims.claim: The hardcoded sigmoid multiplier `6.0_f64` could become too violent if operational velocity changes.
claims.rationale: Changing the curve steepness or binding it to live velocity would alter Minime sensory cadence and semantic persistence. Codex wrote an approval packet instead of retuning the runtime.

claims.claim: Entropy/fill/pressure nested multipliers near the semantic entropy persistence threshold need finite, bounded behavior.
claims.rationale: The existing alternate-shape, handover, and surge tests keep semantic stale windows bounded and monotonic across the reported bands; no live sensory-bus mutation was made.
